title: Portal - Digital Asset Management
sub: Designed a centralized digital asset management platform that enables sales teams to organize, govern, collaborate, and securely share sales collateral from a single workspace—reducing document friction, improving collaboration, and accelerating deal closures.
content:
Overview

Portal is a Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform built to simplify document management throughout the sales lifecycle. As sales teams collaborate with marketing, legal, and product teams, presentations, contracts, proposals, and supporting documents often become scattered across multiple platforms. Portal centralizes these assets into a single workspace, allowing teams to efficiently manage documents, maintain governance, and securely share customer-ready content without disrupting their sales workflow.

The Problem

Sales representatives were spending valuable time searching for the latest version of documents stored across cloud drives, emails, and collaboration tools. Frequent platform switching, duplicate files, inconsistent versioning, and fragmented approval workflows created delays that slowed down deal progression and increased operational overhead.

The Solution

Portal brings document management, collaboration, governance, and secure document sharing into one unified platform. The solution enables teams to manage document lifecycles, collaborate across departments, maintain version history, and create secure Deal Rooms where prospects can access approved sales materials through a private link. A built-in analytics dashboard also provides visibility into document usage and deal progress.

Design Process

Brief

The objective was to design an intuitive platform that streamlines document management for sales teams while reducing collaboration bottlenecks and accelerating deal cycles.

Define

Project goals focused on centralizing sales documents, eliminating version conflicts, simplifying governance, improving collaboration, enabling secure customer sharing, and providing visibility into document performance. The design process followed clearly defined milestones from research and wireframing through prototyping and usability testing using Figma, Notion, and Jira.

Ideation

The project began with creating a Sales Development Representative (SDR) persona to better understand user motivations, pain points, and workflows. This user-centered approach informed every design decision throughout the project. User journey maps exposed friction during document preparation and approvals, while affinity mapping grouped research insights into recurring themes such as poor discoverability, collaboration delays, and version confusion. A Dollar Test helped prioritize features based on user value, confirming that document search, version control, and secure sharing were the most impactful capabilities.

Key Features

AI Document Classifier

Automatically generates metadata, tags, and document categories using AI, making documents significantly easier to search and retrieve while reducing manual organization.

Document Version Control

Maintains complete revision history with clear version tracking, ensuring teams always collaborate on the latest approved document while preserving previous iterations.

Governance Workflow

Introduces structured approval workflows where marketing, legal, and management teams can review, approve, or request revisions directly within the platform before documents reach customers.

Deal Room

Allows sales teams to instantly create secure private links containing presentations, contracts, proposals, and supporting documents. This eliminates repetitive download-upload workflows and significantly reduces context switching between platforms.

Design

A comprehensive UI audit identified inconsistencies in the existing experience, leading to the creation of a scalable design system with standardized typography, spacing, components, icons, and interaction patterns. Information architecture was restructured around user tasks rather than system functions, while iterative wireframing validated navigation flows before moving into high-fidelity designs. The resulting design toolkit improved consistency across design, engineering, and marketing teams.

Outcome

Portal successfully reduced friction across document governance and delivery by centralizing every stage of the sales document lifecycle. Teams were able to retrieve files faster, collaborate more efficiently, eliminate version conflicts, and securely deliver customer-ready documents without relying on multiple disconnected tools.

User Feedback

"The interface feels incredibly organized and intuitive. Finding documents now takes seconds instead of minutes, and having approvals, version history, and customer sharing all in one place has dramatically improved how our sales team collaborates." — Product Manager, MathCo

What I Learned

This project reinforced the importance of reducing operational friction in enterprise software. I learned how immediate access to reliable information and stronger communication between cross-functional teams can significantly improve the efficiency of a sales pipeline. Designing an integrated workflow instead of isolated features also demonstrated how consolidating multiple tools into one platform reduces cognitive load and helps sales teams focus on closing deals rather than managing documents.

Missed Opportunities

Although the initial scope included developing a complete visual identity with custom iconography, typography, and a dedicated brand asset library, these initiatives were postponed due to project timelines. Expanding the design system into a comprehensive brand language remains a valuable opportunity for future iterations.

Moving Forward

As Portal continues to onboard new customers, future iterations will leverage anonymous product analytics and user feedback to refine workflows, introduce additional automation, and further improve the overall experience while supporting increasingly complex enterprise sales operations.

title: Forma — Material Intelligence
sub: An AI-assisted material selection tool for sustainable architecture
content:
Background

Architecture firms spend an average of 340 hours per project on material research and specification. Forma reduces this to under 40 hours while simultaneously improving sustainability outcomes and cost predictability.

Core Challenge

Material selection involves thousands of interdependent variables: structural performance, embodied carbon, cost trajectories, lead times, aesthetic coherence, and regulatory compliance. No single expert can hold all of this in their head. Forma makes it possible.

Intelligence Layer

The underlying model was trained on 2.3 million material specifications, 180,000 completed projects, and real-time pricing from 600 suppliers. It understands context — a coastal building faces different material constraints than an inland one, even if the programs are identical.

Interface Design

Rather than surfacing a list of options, Forma surfaces a field of possibilities — a spatial canvas where materials cluster by compatibility, sustainability profile, and cost band. Architects explore by zooming, filtering, and comparing, not by navigating menus.

Collaboration Features

A shared project space allows structural engineers, interior designers, and clients to annotate the material field together. Decisions are logged with reasoning, creating a searchable specification history that survives personnel changes.

Impact

Used by 340 architecture firms in 28 countries. Average embodied carbon reduction: 23% compared to traditional selection methods. Average specification time reduction: 88%.
